commit.message:
Claude/pull latest changes vn fh a (#21)

* Fix seed.ts import to use custom Prisma Client output path

The Prisma schema generates the client to src/generated/prisma, but
seed.ts was importing from @prisma/client which is empty. Updated the
import to use the correct custom output path.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XeZEcvffGtMeyUPMXQFxrb

* Fix seed.ts import to use @/ path alias for Prisma client

The relative path ../src/generated/prisma wasn't resolving correctly
with tsx. Using the @/generated/prisma path alias from tsconfig instead.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XeZEcvffGtMeyUPMXQFxrb

* Remove custom Prisma output path, use default @prisma/client import

The custom output path (src/generated/prisma) caused module resolution
failures with tsx when running the seed. Removing it so Prisma generates
to the default location and all imports use @prisma/client.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XeZEcvffGtMeyUPMXQFxrb

* Restore Prisma custom output and fix seed command for ESM resolution

- prisma-client provider requires an output path, restored it
- Changed seed command from "npx tsx" to "node --import tsx" to run
  in ESM mode, fixing module resolution for the custom output path
- Reverted imports to use the correct relative paths to generated client

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XeZEcvffGtMeyUPMXQFxrb

* Add required output path for prisma-client generator

prisma-client provider requires an explicit output path. Added it back
along with the correct import in src/lib/prisma.ts.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XeZEcvffGtMeyUPMXQFxrb

* Fix seed.ts import to reference client.js entry point directly

The generated Prisma client directory has no index.ts/index.js, so
Node/tsx cannot resolve a bare directory import. Point to client.js
explicitly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XeZEcvffGtMeyUPMXQFxrb

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Save responses next to input file for OneDrive sync

Output is now responses_<input_stem>.xlsx in the same folder as the
input .xls file, so it syncs automatically via OneDrive without
relying on AppData which doesn't persist between hospital sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: robust crash recovery after session revert/reset

Three fixes to prevent 'exited with code 1' crashes after revert:

1. Revert flow now interrupts the old session's Claude process before
   creating a new one. Previously old processes kept running and
   conflicted with new ones (causing accumulated channels).

2. Backend retry now triggers on ANY 'exited with code 1' error,
   not just when a resume ID was set. Fresh sessions that crash
   on first attempt now also get an automatic retry.

3. editAndRestart fallback clears sessionId BEFORE restartClaude
   (from previous commit, preserved here).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Zanele ref image 400 error and redirect dual-scene gaze to interpersonal

Root cause: Zanele's stored_url pointed to a missing Supabase file (HTTP 400),
so her reference image silently failed to load — Kontext generated without any
identity reference for her.

Fixes:
- Add fetchRefImageBase64() helper that tries stored_url first, then falls
  back to sfw_url if it fails (instead of giving up on first failure)
- Apply fallback to all three ref image fetch paths (single face+body,
  dual primary, dual secondary)
- For dual-character scenes, auto-replace "looking directly at camera" with
  "looking at the other person" so interacting characters face each other
- Fixed Zanele's broken stored_url in Supabase DB
- Fixed 5 Lobola List dual-scene prompts: camera gaze → interpersonal gaze

commit.message:
[AI Context] Expand test suite to 108 assertions across 19 categories

Add 6 new test categories: symlinks (entity symlinks are a known limitation
of find -type d, subpath and pointer symlinks work), very long paths (deep
nesting resolves correctly), non-markdown files (.yaml/.txt/.json ignored),
multiple pointers to same target (different relative paths computed correctly),
extra frontmatter fields (unrelated YAML, comments, colons in values),
and awk replacement edge cases (content preservation, multiple canonical
lines, special characters, frontmatter intact after update).

All 108 assertions pass. No script changes needed — no new bugs found.

the package name: AV_. Clean break with no deprecation fallback.

shell scripts, Docker configs, and documentation. Protocol payload type
constants (VCAV_E_INVITE_V1, VCAV_RELAY_INBOX_V1) are unchanged as
they are wire-format identifiers, not configuration.

feat: add GitHub webhook endpoint for issue-triggered pipelines (Design 3A) (#47)

New webhook endpoint at POST /api/webhooks/github that triggers Forge
pipelines from GitHub issue comments containing /forge. Includes HMAC-SHA256
signature verification, collaborator gating via author_association, repo
allow-list, rate limiting (1 pipeline/issue/5 min), and background pipeline
orchestration with progress comments posted back to the issue.

Changes across 7 files:
- webhooks.py: Full endpoint + helpers (_verify_signature, _is_collaborator,
  _extract_forge_command, _build_task_description, _check_rate_limit,
  _post_issue_comment, _run_webhook_pipeline)
- webhooks_test.py: 49 tests covering all helpers, endpoint status codes,
  DB integration, pipeline lifecycle, and history field propagation
- app.py: Register webhooks router + set webhook app.state from ForgeSettings
- settings.py: Add github_webhook_secret, github_allowed_repos,
  github_webhook_project_dir
- db.py: Add github_issue_url/github_issue_number to PipelineRow + create_pipeline
- history.py: Include github issue fields in list + detail responses
- tasks.py: Add issue_number param to _auto_create_pr for Closes #N in PR body

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Report agent version in heartbeats

Previously agent_version was only set during registration, which
fails on restart because the agent sends its device key but the
registration endpoint requires the master key. Now the agent
includes its version in every heartbeat, and the server persists
it to the device record. Version appears immediately after the
first heartbeat, no re-registration needed.

commit.message:
feat: store arxiv_id + open_access_url in graph, support HTML article downloads

The graph builder now persists arxiv_id (from S2 externalIds) and
open_access_url (for non-arxiv papers like Distill and Transformer
Circuits Thread) on every node. The download pipeline prefers arxiv
PDF URLs constructed from stored IDs (no live API call), falls back
to open_access_url, then S2 API. HTML articles are saved alongside
PDFs and the agent prompt handles both formats.

- graph_builder.py: extract arxiv_id + open_access_url from S2 API
- config.py: add openAccessPdf to PAPER_FIELDS
- enrich_arxiv_ids.py: new enrichment script (batch API, 2-3 calls)
- download.py: rewritten with HTML support, logging, content-type handling
- run.py: HTML text extraction for LLM fallback path
- sampling.py: pass arxiv_id + open_access_url through to download
- graph_state.json: enriched (937 arxiv_id, 16 open_access_url)
- question_generator_prompt.txt: updated for PDF + HTML articles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: AutoInterp
repository.description: LLM Framework for Automating Interpretability Research

commit.message:
fix(piece): provide default cause in setupPersistent to avoid createRef warning (#2967)

When creating a new piece, no causal reference was passed to createRef,
triggering a noisy "[createRef] NO CAUSE" warning and stack trace. Now
generates a cause from the space DID and a random UUID when none is
provided, preserving the same behavior (unique piece per call) without
the error log.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add match struct/vector/quoted/and/or/not/apply patterns and defmethod full form

Match patterns now support:
- Struct patterns: (MyStruct a b) with predicate check and field extraction
- Vector patterns: #(a b c) with length check and element binding
- Quoted symbol patterns: 'foo with eq?/eqv?/equal? as appropriate
- And/or/not compound patterns for combining predicates
- Predicate-with-binding: (? pred var) tests and binds
- Apply patterns: (apply func pat) applies func then matches result
- @list patterns from reader's [...] syntax

defmethod now handles the full form:
  (defmethod (name (self Type) args...) body...)
  → (method-set! Type::t 'name (lambda (self args...) body...))
Including => return type annotation stripping.

All 26 new tests + 17 existing compiler tests + 24 tier1 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: editor UX, vault detection, workflow execution, and server slug resolution (v0.3.4)

- Fix transform step expression→condition mapping: add expression field to WorkflowStep,
  engine reads step.expression ?? step.condition for transforms
- Vault auto-detection: editor cross-references env vars with vault credentials,
  shows green/red indicators and "X of Y configured" count
- Editor layout overhaul: remove all shrink-0 constraints, page scrolls naturally,
  sticky sidebar, no right-side cutoff
- Server slug→ID resolution: mcpManager.resolveId() handles both slugs and DB IDs
  so AI-generated workflows (which use slugs) work correctly
- AI planner improvements: flat sequential steps only (no branching), safe expressions,
  correct Brave Search text output documentation, flattenSteps() safety net
- Bump version to v0.3.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix frequency overestimation for superlatives and loanwords

Problems:
- "engsten" (superlative of "eng") was unknown → C1
- "E-Mail" normalized to "email" was unknown → C1

Fixes:
- Add superlative/comparative lemmatization (engsten → eng, größten → groß)
- Add umlaut restoration in lemmatization (ä→a, ö→o, ü→u)
- Add common loanwords list (email, internet, computer, smartphone, etc.)

Now "Als erstes schickt er eine E-Mail an seine tausend engsten Freunde."
correctly shows freq: A1 instead of C1.

commit.message:
fix: restore full desktop OS functionality for main site owner

Main site owner was restricted to free plan (8 system apps only) because
tenant is null on the main site and parsePlanFromSettings defaulted to
"free". This blocked 21 of 29 apps, hid them from dock/spotlight, and
prevented launch.

Changes:
- useBilling: return enterprise plan when isMainSite is true
- DesktopShell: enable onboarding wizard for main site (localStorage-based)
- Spotlight: add 10 missing icons (Terminal, Activity, StickyNote, etc.)
- App.tsx: add routes for 5 orphaned pages (WarRoom, ApiAccess,
  DataManagement, DedicatedInstance, PartnerProgram) and 404 catch-all
- AdminLayout: add sidebar entries and icons for the 5 new routes

commit.message:
fix(tooling): detect thumbs-up-only review completion

When a reviewer gives a thumbs-up on the trigger comment without posting
a formal review (common for trivial changes like applying the reviewer's
own suggestion), the state machine now treats this as "complete (0 issues)"
instead of "pending". Condition: thumbsup > 0 AND no eyes emoji AND no
formal review/comment after the trigger.

Bumps task-lifecycle skill to v3.1.0.
